# 今日はよい風呂の日なのでｺﾚ
柚子香る温もり鶏団子汁

## 材料
鶏ひき肉、長ねぎ、生姜、卵、だし汁、柚子の皮、きのこ、青菜、醤油、塩。柚子の香りと生姜で体温まるご褒美汁に。

## 作り方
鶏ひき肉に刻みねぎ、生姜、卵を混ぜ団子に。だし汁で団子、きのこ、青菜を煮る。醤油、塩で味を調え、仕上げに柚子の皮を散らせば、温かい香りが広がる。

## イメージしたポイント
柚子の香りが湯船の癒しを演出。生姜で体を芯から温め、風呂上がりにホッと安らぐ一杯。心身ともにリラックス。

The Sparkler in the Snow
#AIArt #AIPortrait #Photorealism #AIGenerated #AIPhotography

images.alt: A woman in her early forties stands alone at the edge of a deserted Ferris wheel platform in a closed winter amusement park at dusk. She wears a dark green wool coat with the collar turned up and holds a lit sparkler in her outstretched hand, its white-gold light casting tiny sparks onto fresh snow at her feet. Her face is tilted up with a calm, unreadable expression; her skin shows natural details like fine lines, wind-chapped lips, and a small scar on her left eyebrow. The sparkler’s light reflects in her eyes. Behind her, a rusted Ferris wheel with missing cars stands against a deep indigo sky. In the background are a frozen cotton candy stand, a boarded ticket booth, and snow-covered rides. No footprints approach her. A distant floodlight casts a weak blue-white glow far behind her, mixing with the sparkler’s warm light. Snow crystals on her coat and falling sparks are rendered in crisp detail, with a gentle darkening at the edges of the frame. The image is in color.

I explore this frontier: the power of staging to build an emotional truth that, despite being fabricated, became the eternal symbol of spontaneous love.

The technical precision of AI allows us to reinterpret this language, proving that the impact of an image lies in its deep emotional connection.

For forty years, the world believed in the spontaneity of this kiss until a 90s lawsuit forced Doisneau to admit it was orchestrated. 
The subjects were actors Françoise Bornet and Jacques Carteaud, paid by the photographer. He argued he would never dare photograph real lovers in such a bold manner.

This digital composition pays homage to Robert Doisneau’s aesthetic, capturing a couple kissing in Paris with a focus on the movement and atmosphere of the 20th century.

This piece reflects on a major myth of humanist photography: the "stolen moment" that was actually a meticulously staged scene.

For the record: I DISAGREE with Joseph Campbell's deepest conclusion. I think ultimately in a planet with over 8.2 billion people - our freedom is limited by compassion. The individual has to edu understand anti-greed.

I think Jean and Joseph Erdman  were great at expressing this contradiction. But bottom line: we should listen to what Malala wrote in year 2013 when she was insane with death-threats and wilding with goodness. We should educate ourselves. "Ready Player One" movie gets a lot of mocking, but with 9 billion or 10 billion or 11 billion - it may not be the worst joy and fun we can do in avoiding a real Interstellar 2014 film world war. Over-educated virtual love one another sit in one small shack of a house may actually be a reality of 10 billion people sharing our Planet until we truly become technological ultra-smart peaceful goodness gods.

「そんな感じ、”もしもの私”はペンギンショーのお姉さん。この仔たちね、二足歩行の生き物はみんな自分たちの仲間だと思うんだって」
それ、聞いたことある。
「それじゃ、お姉さん一つ芸をみせてあげるね。これしかできないんだけど……」
そう言ってうちの子が指をパチンと鳴らした。すると、うちの子に向かってペンギンたちが一斉に上をむき、背筋を伸ばして羽根を広げるポーズを見せた。
「えへへ、かわいいでしょ」
そのポーズって確か意味があったような
